name: foolproof-ux
title: app-factory dev-server — foolproof operator UX
intent: At every moment a sloppy user can answer: (a) is it connected, (b) what can I do now and why, (c) is something running and how far along, (d) did it succeed or fail and why, (e) where is the result — without reading a terminal. And the surface can neither mislead about what it does nor corrupt state.

dimensions.description: Liveness, loading, and empty states are explicit — blank is never ambiguous (broken vs. empty).
The user knows what they can do right now and exactly why an action is unavailable.
A run reports its steps as they happen; status is observable from any tab and survives reload (server-side).
Success and failure are clear and friendly — a terminal result with counts, a plain-language reason for a block.
The user can see what was actually produced — the build artifacts, viewable read-only.
No double-runs and no state corruption — a per-project run lock; reset refused mid-run.
The UI never claims the stdlib server builds with AI; it validates the frontier and routes real building to a Claude Code session.
Edits on disk refresh the view; bad requests return friendly JSON, never a stack; reset re-arms the demo.
